DISCOVERY 525, PLAINTIFF LUZ ESTELA DE MUNOZ'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY AND FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE COURT AWARDS SANCTIONS TO PLAINTIFFS AND AGAINST DEFENDANT RAMIREZ IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,720.00, PAYABLE NO LATER THAN 10 DAYS AFTER THE DATE OF THE ORDER ENTERED HEREIN. (SEE MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS IS DIRECTED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND SUBMIT IT TO PSW@HASSARD.COM FOR REVIEW AND SIGNATURE. MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-22-2025 Continued To Nov-10-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-01-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Jonathan Joiner, Esq., HUMPHREYS JOINER LAW GROUP, LLP (415) 528-2929 jj@humphreysjoiner.com
Attorneys for Plaintiffs, LUZ ESTELA ALVAREZ DE MUNOZ; EVERARDO MUNOZ; GISSELLE MUNOZ, a minor, by and through her Guardian Ad Litem
No other appearances.

ALL PARTIES PRESENT STIPULATE TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM DECIDING TODAY'S MOTION. THE COURT CLERK IS AUTHORIZED TO SIGN THE SIPULATION ON PARTIES BEHALF.

Hearing is held. Disscussed, not ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling with modifcation as to the amount of sanctions as follows:

PLAINTIFF LUZ ESTELA DE MUNOZ'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions

This is a personal injury action brought by the plaintiff family for wrongful constructive eviction and damages arising from their occupation of a residential unit on Mission Street. Plaintiffs also complain about defendants' failure to refund their security deposit upon vacating the premises.
In January of this year, plaintiffs served written discovery upon, among others, defendant Ruben Ramirez who is alleged to be one of the current landlords for the subject property and the proprietor/operator of a taqueria on the ground floor of the mixed use building in which plaintiffs resided. That discovery consisted of a set of form interrogatories, a set of special interrogatories, a request for production of documents and a request for admissions. Despite several attempts by plaintiff's counsel to obtain responses through defendant Ramirez's attorney, no responses (timely or otherwise) were received. Thereafter, on August 13, 2024 plaintiffs filed two motions now before the Court: a motion for an order compelling defendant Ramirez to respond to the interrogatories and request for production of documents and a motion for an order deeming admitted the facts addressed in the Request for Admissions. Plaintiffs also sought sanctions in both motions. The sanctions request is, however, confusing in that plaintiffs seek sanctions in the amount of $2,720 in their first motion and then both the $2,720 and another $1,120 in their second.
Defendant Ramirez does not oppose the Court granting plaintiffs' first motion. On August 27, 2024, he served responses to the interrogatories and document request. For reasons set forth in his response, he contends the award of sanctions would be "unjust". On that same date, he also served responses to the Request for Admissions and, in view of his now-served responses, opposes the motion to deem the requests admitted.
The Court will, therefore, GRANT plaintiffs' motion for an order compelling defendant Ramirez's responses to the interrogatory sets and the request for production of documents. For the reasons set forth in defendant Ramirez's responsive papers, the sanctions request is DENIED. As for the motion for an order deeming admitted the Request for Admissions, that motion is DENIED based on the late-served (but pre-hearing) responses and Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.280(c). On the subject of sanctions, the cited provision of the Code of Civil Procedure mandates an award of sanctions in a matter of this kind. Accordingly, the Court awards sanctions to plaintiffs and against defendant Ramirez in the amount of $2.720.00, payable no later than 10 days after the date of the order entered herein.
Counsel for plaintiffs is directed to prepare a proposed order consistent with the Court's ruling and submit it to psw@hassard.com for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (525/JPT)

Plaintiffs also sought sanctions in both motions. The sanctions request is, however, confusing in that plaintiffs seek sanctions in the amount of $2,720 in their first motion and then both the $2,720 and another $1,120 in their second.

On the subject of sanctions, the cited provision of the Code of Civil Procedure mandates an award of sanctions in a matter of this kind. Counsel for plaintiffs is directed to prepare a proposed order consistent with the Court's ruling and submit it to psw@hassard.com for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (525/JPT)
